[165:11]And there's a very interesting article today in the Wall Street Journal.

[165:14]It's an editorial and it talks about the mass deportations.

[165:18]And like I said, at the top of the show, there has been so much said about mass deportations

[165:23]from a cultural and political lens.

[165:26]When we think about immigration, what we really think about is a cultural social issue.

[165:32]We think about a largely white majority, an increasingly small white majority, that is

[165:39]frustrated with the influx of immigrants who have a different culture, speak a different

[165:44]language, have created this underclass lifestyle where they're dependent on welfare, where

[165:51]they never assimilate.

[165:55]the texture of life of most cities in America because of these ethnic enclaves they're creating,

[166:01]they're browning America. And this is how we typically think about it.

[166:05]And so when we think about illegal immigration, we think about issues of society.

[166:12]Should these people stay in our country?

[166:14]Are they contributing?

[166:16]Are they good people?

[166:18]Are they real Americans?

[166:20]What does it mean to be American?
